content.full_text:
April 1, 1977


Mr. Burt Schwarzschild
CREATE LOBBY
P. O. Box 14166
San Francisco, California  94114

Dear Mr. Schwarzchild:

Thank you for your letter of March 24, 1977 regarding your
status as a lobbyist. Mr. Eichman has recently left the
Commission staff, so I have been asked to respond to you.

A person does not meet the definition of a lobbyist under the
Political Reform Act unless a substantial or regular portion of
the activities for which he or she receives consideration is for
the purpose of influencing legislative or administrative action.
For the purpose of defining the terms "substantial or regular,"
the Commission in 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18239(e), established
certain tests and compensation tests as fully described in
Section II-A of the lobbyist instruction manual.

In your letter it is stated that you do not meet, nor expect to
meet those time tests. Therefore, until such time that you meet the
time or compensation tests, you will not be required to register as
a lobbyist. However, it should be noted that any person who makes
payments to influence legislative or administrative action, as defined
in Section 82045 of the Political Reform Act, of $250 or more in
any month will be required to file a report, even though that person
is not a lobbyist. This filing obligation is more fully described
in the enclosed fact sheets.

Please do not hesitate to contact me if I can be of further
assistance.

Sincerely,


ALAN L. HERNDON
Enclosures                Special Compliance Representative

CREATE Lobby
                                           Clean, Reliable, Efficient Al[illegible]ate Transit and Energy Lobby
                                           PO Box 14166 • San Francisco, CA 94114 • (415) 864-4249
                                                                                                     381-2600

March 24, 1977

FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
1100 "K" Street
Sacramento CA 95814

Att: Rich Eichman, Technical Assistance & Analysis Div.

Dear Mr. Eichman:

After reading your letter of March 1, and your enclosed "Instruction Manual for Lobbyists",
I believe that I am not a lobbyist as defined in the Manual or in the Political
Reform Act of 1974 for these reasons:

1. I do not receive any salary or compensation for time, or effort spent on behalf of
CREATE Lobby, nor does CREATE Lobby. I as its owner/plan to pay me or any other
persons,salary or other compensation for lobbying activities.

2. I have to date received no compensation for travel expenses; I personally have
absorbed these expenses. If CREATE requests become adequate, I may permit the reimbursement of some travel expenses associated with CREATE Lobby's activities, such as bus,
plane, train tickets, etc. According to your enclosure, the payment of "reasonable
travel expenses" is not one of the croteria defining the term, "lobbyist" (IIA).

3. CREATE's expenses and payments so far have been for printing, postage, and processing
of mailings to CREATE supporters and contributors, or prospective supporters and
contributors. The intent of these mailings ase been both to inform and to solicit
support,and contributions to pay for these expenses. To date, contributions have been
far inadequate to pay these expenses; future contributions received will be used to
pay these obligations, and if they can be paid off, to develop some reserve for
communicating with the CREATE supporters and to inform them about developments in
the Transit & Energy field.

I do not understand how my letter of Feb. 20, implies that I am, "....receiving funds
or other than reimbursement for reasonable travel expenses (your letter)". or that
I am, "...receiving oeconomic consideration..." While it is true that in my CREATE
information sheet, I indicated that financial support was needed to, "...meet printing,
mailing, travel, personnel, and other expenses...", the low level of financial support
which materialized, precluded the possibility of paying any salary or compensation
to myself or anyone else.

4. Since I hold down a regular job as sales engineer for the RDX Corp., and have
received no salary or other compensation to date, nor plan to receive any from CREATE
contributions, other than occasional payment for direct travel costs, I can only
spend a limited time on CREATE related activities. I therefore do not meet, nor expect
to meet the time tests outlined under Section II-4.

I therefore ask you to reevaluate my status. I hope that in the light of this
information, you will agree that I am not a "lobbyist" as defined in the material
you sent me.

I look forward to your reply.

Sincerely,

CREATE Lobby

[signature]

State of California

Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 · SACRAMENTO, 95804 · · · 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

March 1, 1977


Bert Schwarzschild
CREATE LOBBY
P.O. Box 14166
San Francisco, CA    94114

Dear Mr. Schwarzschild:

     Thank you for your letter of February 20, 1977 in which
you ask whether you are required to register as a lobbyist.

     Section 82039 of the Political Reform Act of 1974
defines a lobbyist as "any person who is employed or
contracts for economic consideration, other than reimburse-
ment for reasonable travel expenses, to communicate directly
or through his agents with any elective state official, agency
agency official or legislative official for the purpose of
influencing legislative or administrative action, if a
substantial or regular portion of the activities for which
he receives consideration is for the purpose of influencing
legislative or administrative action. No person is a
lobbyist by reason of activities described in Section 86300."
According to your letter you are receiving funds for "other
than reimbursement for reasonable travel expenses". Our
staff has determined that in this particular situation you
are receiving economic consideration and may be required to
register as a lobbyist if you meet any one of the time or
compensation tests defined in 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section
1823'9(e). Those tests are described in the enclosed Instruction
Manual for Lobbyists.

     Please contact this office if we can be of further
assistance.

                    Sincerely,

                    [signature]
                    Rich Eichman
                    Special Compliance Representative

RE:av

Enclosure (1)

CREATE Lobby
Clean, Reliable, Efficient Alternate Transit and Energy Lobby
PO Box 14166 • San Francisco, CA 94114 • (415) 864-4249

20 February 1977

FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
1100 "K" Street
Sacramento CA 95814

Att: Rich Eichman, Technical Assistance & Analysis Div.

Dear Mr. Eichman:

This confirms my phone call to you seeking your opinion as to whether my one-man CREATE lobby which I recently organized and now operate, must register as a lobbying organization and/or whether I must register as a lobbyist.

CREATE is a "fictitious name" sole proprietorship registered with the San Francisco County Clerk. It speaks on behalf of citizens who authorize it to lobby on their behalf for Clean Reliable Efficient Alternate Transit & Energy (see enclosure). In collecting this authorization, I also request voluntary contributions to support this effort, i.e. pay for "....printing, mailing, travel, personnel, and other expenses." Thus far the money received has been used solely to pay for printing and mailing, and phone expenses. I envisage some of the antitrust money will be used to pay travel expenses associated with the lobbying effort. I don't anticipate paying salary to myself or others.

Please advise me in writing if I/and/or CREATE need to register as a Lobbyist.

Sincerely,

Bart Schwarzschild

CREATE Lobby Clean, Reliable, Efficient Alternate Transit and Energy Lobby
PO Box 14166 • San Francisco, CA 94114 • (415) 861-2600

CREATE Lobby is working on behalf of more than 10,000 Californians for Clean, Reliable, Efficient Alternate Transit and Energy.

CREATE Lobby supports a statewide transit system that will provide every Californian with an effective, viable transit alternative to the automobile.

CREATE Lobby opposes the efforts of the "Highway Lobby" (oil, auto, road construction industries, trucking and auto associations, and other vested interests) to increase our dependence on the auto.

CREATE Lobby supports an intensive effort to develop "clean" energy sources such as solar, geo-thermal, wind, and fusion, in order to reduce environmental pollution that our present power sources create.

CREATE Lobby supports an intensive public and government effort to conserve the tremendous energy resources that government, industry, and the public waste each day.

CREATE Lobby communicates with its California constituents about transit and energy matters by:
• periodic regional meetings
• periodic newsletters
• individual contact

CREATE Lobby urges you to support this vital lobbying effort by:
• authorizing a CREATE Lobbyist to lobby for the above goals on your behalf for one year.
• contributing the needed financial support to sustain CREATE's lobbying effort—to meet printing, mailing, travel, personnel, and other expenses.

If you support CREATE's objectives, please mail the attached coupon with your contribution.

Sincerely, CREATE Lobby

Bert Schwarzschild

Bert Schwarzschild has fought for consumer and civil rights for many years. In San Francisco, he has led such successful efforts as the reconstruction of a beautiful, pedestrian-oriented Upper Market Street and the creation of a unique, community-conceived park. In 1971, he organized and led a reform effort within the California State Automobile Association. He became the first opposition candidate for AAA director in the U.S. He holds proxies from 10,000 auto club members who support an expanded public transit system. He is a Director of American Youth Hostel, G.G. Council; an engineer; and Navy veteran.

Clip and mail to: CREATE Lobby, Att., Bert Schwarzschild
P.O. Box 14166, San Francisco, CA 94114.

LOBBYING AUTHORIZATION
(One coupon per individual or household—additional coupons available.)

The undersigned, who is also a registered California voter/s, hereby authorize/s CREATE Lobby to lobby on my/our behalf for Clean, Reliable, Efficient, Alternate Transit and Energy.

1) _________________ _________________ _______
PRINT NAME SIGNATURE DATE

2) _________________ _________________ _______
PRINT NAME SIGNATURE DATE

3) _________________ _________________ _______
PRINT NAME SIGNATURE DATE

4) _________________ _________________ _______
PRINT NAME SIGNATURE DATE

_________________ _________________ _______
ADDRESS OF UNDERSIGNED CITY STATE ZIP CODE

$_______Contribution enclosed to sustain CREATE Lobby. (Suggested: Regular Support: $10
Single/$15 Household; Super Support: $20 Single/$25 Household; Minimum or Student Support: $5 Single/$8 Household.

CREATE Lobby    Clean, Reliable, Efficient Alternate Transit and Energy Lobby
PO Box 14166 • San Francisco, CA 94114 • (415) 861-2600

February, 1977

Dear Friend:

Thank you for assigning your proxy to me! I am unable to respond personally to the thousands of proxies coming in, so please excuse a form letter and occasional duplicate mailing. Since the board-controlled by-laws limited my Statement of Candidacy in "Motorland" to 50 words, I would like to bring you up to date about the "CSAA Reform" effort and about CREATE Lobby.

For five years (since 1971), I and other concerned members of the California State Auto Association have attempted to bring about much-needed reform within the auto club. I, and other auto club members, had organized "Members for CSAA Reform" to try to bring about these reforms. For five years, we urged the CSAA Board of Directors and its management to develop greater sensitivity for our threatened environment; to advocate safer and more efficient cars and alternate modes of transit; and to permit membership control.

The outpouring of 10,000 proxies of concerned auto club members, and the suggestions, requests, cajoling, demands, and lawsuits by our group of reform-oriented members hardly affected the calcified attitudes of the 24 CSAA directors who run the Auto Association like a personal fiefdom. Other than giving some token lip-service to "balanced transportation," and to environmental concern, in "Motorland" magazine, the board has continued its "love affair" with the Highway Lobby.

I have become convinced that as long as the present board is in power, no meaningful change in the board's pro-Highway Lobby attitude will take place. Since the board controls the election machinery, no other candidates than themselves or their "carbon copy" replacements (appointed when one of them gets too crotchety or dies) will ever be elected. So we have a classic "Catch 22" situation! The purpose of my candidacy was to try to bring to the members' attention this undemocratic stranglehold by the board on association affairs which excludes members from having a meaningful voice in their association. While this condition persists, neither I nor any other rank-and-file candidate can ever be elected.

CREATE Lobby was conceived from a realization that our advocacy for viable transit (and energy) alternatives could be voiced more positively and effectively in the public arena, instead of in the corridors and meeting halls of the auto club. CREATE Lobby's main goal is contained in its very name: Clean, Reliable, Efficient, Alternate Transit and Energy. We are lobbying for this goal in the State Legislature, at public hearings, before citizens groups, through publications, and in other media.

I invite you to become a CREATE supporter. Please read, fill in, and return the Lobbying Authorization coupon, and pass the extra copy to an interested friend. Your immediate support will strengthen CREATE Lobby in its effort to achieve the exciting transit and energy breakthroughs that are needed, and possible, in California.

Sincerely,

CREATE Lobby

[signature]

Bert Schwarzschild
BS:cr

P.S.: Please join me and other concerned CSAA members at the auto club's annual meeting, Thursday, February 24, 1:00 p.m., at the Mark Hopkins Hotel in San Francisco, if you can. We need friendly faces to counter the horde of CSAA supervisory "robots" who dutifully attend and vote the management line. You must see it to believe it: The CSAA "Annual Circus!"

CREATE Lobby    Clean, Reliable, Efficient Alternate Transit and Energy Lobby
PO Box 14166 • San Francisco, CA 94114 • (415) 861-2600


January 4, 1977

Dear Friend:

Since 1971, you and I, and 10,000 other concerned members of the California
State Auto Association, have attempted to bring about much needed reform
within the auto club. I, and other auto club members had organized "Members
for CSAA Reform" to try to bring about these reforms. For 5 years, we urged
the CSAA Board of Directors and its management to develop greater sensitivity
for our threatened environment; to advocate safer and more efficient cars and
alternate modes of transit; and to permit membership control.

The outpouring of 10,000 proxies of concerned auto club members, and the
suggestions, requests, cajoling, demands, and lawsuit by our group of reform-
oriented members hardly affected the calcified attitudes of the 24 CSAA
Directors who run the Auto Association like a personal fiefdom. Other than
giving some token lip-service to "balanced transportation", and to environ-
mental concern, in "Motorland" magazine, the Board has continued its "love
affair" with the Highway Lobby.

CREATE Lobby was conceived from a realization that our advocacy for viable
transit (and energy) alternatives could be voiced more positively and effec-
tively in the Public Arena, instead of in the corridors and meeting halls of
the auto club. CREATE Lobby's main goal is contained in its very name:
Clean, Reliable, Efficient, Alternate Transit and Energy. We are lobbying
for this goal in the State Legislature, at public hearings, before citizens
groups, through publications, and in the media.

I invite you to become a CREATEsupporter. Please read, fill in, and return
the Lobbying Authorization coupon, and pass the extra copy to an interested
friend. Your immediate support will strengthen CREATE Lobby in its effort
to achieve the exciting transit and energy breakthroughs that are needed, and
possible, in California.

Sincerely,

CREATE Lobby

[signature]
Bert Schwarzschild

BS:da

P.S. I am keeping the proverbial "thorn" in the CSAA Board's side by again
running for election as an opposition candidate, i.e. candidate by
Petition, to the Board of Directors. Watch for the current January-
February 1977 issue of "Motorland" (page 17) for my Statement of Views.
Please join me and other concerned CSAA members at the auto club's
Annual Meeting, Thursday, February 24, 1:00P.M., at San Francisco's
Mark Hopkins Hotel, if you can. We need friendly faces to counter the
horde of CSAA supervisory "robots" who dutifully attend and vote the
management line. You must see it to believe it: The CSAA "Annual Circus"!
